title: Avoid identical branch implementations
description: When all branches in a conditional structure have exactly the same implementation, it indicates redundant logic or a potential design flaw. This makes the conditional structure unnecessary and can confuse maintainers.
recommendation: Remove unnecessary conditional structures or implement different logic for each branch.

noncompliant.description: All branches execute identical code, making the conditional structure redundant.

compliant.description: Either remove redundant conditionals or implement different logic for each branch.
